title: Canonical Project Record (CPR) — root
description: The single, reusable source of truth for one commercial-construction or job-order-contracting (JOC) project. Built by normalizing the verbatim project-intake dossier (which OCR4 produced) into stable, CSI MasterFormat-aligned, provenance-carrying sections (MasterFormat is the sole classification system; its two-digit division is the universal sort key). It is human-readable and is rendered into an interlinked Excel workbook (dashboard + sub-pages), but its purpose is to be machine-consumable: estimating, budgeting, scheduling, buyout, RFI/submittal, safety, and logistics tools all PULL from it instead of re-parsing documents. This layer normalizes and organizes only — it never prices, levels, scores, or decides; that reasoning belongs to the downstream tools.

schema_version.description: Version of THIS canonical model contract. Downstream tools pin to it.

model_version.description: Monotonic revision of this project's record; bumps each time the model is rebuilt as documents/addenda arrive.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the model was assembled (supplied by the caller).

source.description: What this model was built from — the upstream project-intake handoff. The model never re-OCRs; it consumes the dossier extracts and raw envelopes.

dossier_dir.description: Path to the projects/<slug>/ dossier this model was normalized from.

handoff.description: Path to the project-intake handoff.json consumed.

project.description: Identity summary, denormalized from the project_identity section for quick lookup by every consumer.

is_joc.description: True for job-order-contracting / IDIQ task-order work.

classification.description: How this record is organized. CSI MasterFormat is the SOLE classification system — its two-digit division is the universal sort/grouping key for every section and every workbook sheet.

joc_unit_price_book.description: For JOC: the UPB the line items reference (e.g. 'RSMeans/Gordian 2025 City Cost'). Empty for non-JOC.

sections.description: The normalized record. Each key is one canonical section, conforming to its own schema in this folder. A section may be embedded inline (object) or referenced by relative path (string) when the assembler writes sections to separate files. Keys absent from the documents are omitted, not faked.

project_identity.description: project-identity.schema.json — project bio, bid milestones, bid documents, JOC/IDIQ.

contacts.description: contacts.schema.json — project team roster (POC, owner PM, A/E, inspections, AHJ, JOC admin, NTXP estimator).

quick_links.description: quick-links.schema.json — quick-reference table (doc title, page, OCR4 screenshot URL) by category/trade/cost code.

scope.description: scope.schema.json — the master scope register (rows for everything else).

quantity_takeoff.description: quantity-takeoff.schema.json — Summary QTO; the single source of truth other sections pull quantities from.

estimate_sov.description: estimate-sov.schema.json — estimate structure + Schedule of Values + JOC line-item proposal.

budget.description: budget.schema.json — per-trade budget lines (qty pulled from QTO) + editable high-level rollup.

trades.description: trades.schema.json — trade / bid-package breakdown with scope summary, exclusions, clarifications, spec notes.

subcontractors.description: subcontractors.schema.json — subs & suppliers directory (the entity table other sections reference by party_id).

bid_log.description: bid-log.schema.json — per-trade estimated budget + confidence/variance, plus invited/responded bidders and their actual bids.

submittal_log.description: submittal-log.schema.json — submittal register with manufacturer lead times.

critical_path.description: critical-path.schema.json — ranked critical-path items with explanations and accelerating/de-risking alternates.

schedule.description: schedule.schema.json — NTXP project schedule: milestones & tasks.

counts.description: At-a-glance population of each section, so a consumer knows what is worth fetching.

confidence.description: Roll-up of extraction/normalization confidence, overall and per section (0..1).

normalization_log.description: Notable cross-document transformations applied while building the model (entity merges, unit conversions, lump-sum allocations, conflict resolutions). Auditable trail of everything that changed from verbatim.

conflicts.description: Where two documents disagreed on the same fact (e.g. two different bid-due dates, two square-footages). Recorded with both values and their provenance; NOT silently resolved — flagged for a human/downstream tool.

needs_human_review.description: Low-confidence values, unresolved conflicts, failed sections, and gaps a downstream tool should not trust blindly.

next_consumers.description: The tools/skills this record is built to feed (advisory). e.g. vendor-bid-leveling, rfp-analysis, estimating, scheduling, invoice-reconciliation.
